│                       ▐▛███▜▌                      │ What's new                                                                                                                                      │
│                      ▝▜█████▛▘                     │ Fixed single-segment `dir/**` allow rules like `Edit(src/**)` auto-approving writes to nested `dir/` directories anywhere in the tree instead … │
│                        ▘▘ ▝▝                       │ Fixed a permission-check bypass affecting commands run in Windows PowerShell 5.1 sessions                                                       │
│      Fable 5 with high effort · Claude Max ·       │ Fixed Bash permission checks to fail closed on file-descriptor redirect forms that bash parses differently than the permission analyzer         │
│      poul@kjeldager.com's Organization             │ /release-notes for more                                                                                                                         │
│              /tmp/pks-usage-diag-j0zT              │                                                                                                                                                 │

   Settings  Status   Config   Usage   Stats

   Session

   Total cost:            $0.0000
   Total duration (API):  0s
   Total duration (wall): 31s
   Total code changes:    0 lines added, 0 lines removed
   Usage:                 0 input, 0 output, 0 cache read, 0 cache write

   Current session
   ████████████████████████████▉                      58% used
   Resets 4pm (UTC)

   Current week (all models)
   ██████████████████████████████                     60% used
   Resets Jul 20, 4am (UTC)
   +50% weekly limits promo through Jul 19 · clau.de/cc-july-promo

   Current week (Fable)
   ██████████████████████████████████▌                69% used
   Resets Jul 20, 4am (UTC)

   What's contributing to your limits usage?
   Approximate, based on local sessions on this machine — does not include other devices or claude.ai

   Last 24h · these are independent characteristics of your usage, not a breakdown

   88% of your usage came from subagent-heavy sessions
    Each subagent runs its own requests. Be deliberate about spawning them — and
    consider configuring a cheaper model for simpler subagents.

   59% of your usage was while 4+ sessions ran in parallel
    All sessions share one limit. If you don't need them all at once, queueing uses
    it more evenly.

   52% of your usage was at >150k context
    Longer sessions are more expensive even when cached. /compact mid-task, /clear
    when switching to new tasks.

   38% of your usage came from sessions active for 8+ hours
    These are often background/loop sessions. Continuous usage can add up quickly
    so make sure it is intentional.

   32% of your usage came from subagents under "workflow-subagent"
    If this runs frequently, consider configuring its subagents with a cheaper
    model or tightening their prompts.

   Skills                  % of usage
   /run                            2%
   /browser-use                    1%

   Subagents               % of usage
   workflow-subagent              32%
   deep-research                  10%
   Explore                         2%
   general-purpose                 1%

   MCP servers             % of usage
   aspire                          4%

   d to day · w to week

   Usage credits
   Usage credits are off · /usage-credits to turn them on

   Esc to cancel
